title: ranunculus acris subsp. nipponicus
intro: Ranunculus acris subsp. nipponicus, also known as the Japanese buttercup, is a subspecies of the Ranunculus acris plant, a type of flowering plant in the buttercup family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its ability to stimulate circulation, reduce pain and swelling, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Ranunculus acris subsp. nipponicus can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to promote overall health and well-being, with benefits including reduced stress and improved immune function.
